content.full_text:
Fern Polrrrcnr- PRACTrcss CoMMrssroN
-128 J Street r Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95gl"t_2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
August 3, 2010
Ariel Piene Calonne
City Attorney
501 Poli Streer
Ventura, CA 93002-0099
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our file No. I-10-095
Dear Mr. Calonne:
This letter responds to your reqllest for advice on behalf of ventura City councilmember
James L' Monahan regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions of the political Reform Act (the
"Act").1 Please note that oui advice is based solely on ttre provisions of the Act. We therefore
offer no opinion on the application, if any, of othei conflict-of-interest laws such as common law
conflict of interest or Government Code Section 1090.
QUESTION
Does Ventura City Councilmember James Monahan have a conflict of interest that would
bar him from participating in governmental decisions that involve both annexing a parcel of
property to an existing city project (and other decisions regarding that annexation) where his
property is over three miles from the potential annex?
CONCLUSION
No' Based on the facts you provided in both your letter and our telephone conversations,
Mr' Monahan's economic interest in his real property do., not bar him from participating in
these decisions.
FACTS
You are the City Attorney for the City of Ventura and you write with the consent of City
Council Member James L. Monahan. Ventura is engagecl in an on-going project including two
' The Political Retorm Act is contained in Government code Secti0ns g 1000 through g l0l.l. All statLrtory
ref'erencesarettltheGovernmentCotle,unlessotherw'iseindicated. Theregulations()ftheFairp0litical practices
Commission are contairred in Sections lti I l0 through 18997 of Title 2 of thi Calitirrnia Code of Regulations. ,\ll
re-rlulatory ret'erences are to Title 2, Division 6 of the Calitilrnia code of Re-uulations. unless otherwise indicated.


File No. I-10-095
Page No. 2
distinct communities, the westside and North Avenue Community plan project (the ,.project,,).
The Project will establish new planning and zoning regulations for the areas within its
boundaries by creating a "form-based" zoning codi. iuring our telephone conversations, you
explained that "form-based" zoning is made on more of a lo1-by-lot basis than area-by-area in
most cases.
The city council will be deciding whether to support or oppose the area,s LAFCO,s
decision regarding annexing a parcel olland into the eioiect. The parcel in whole is about g00
acres and the city has already annexed 120 of those acres. Once the LAFCO decides whether to
anlex the property (called "Caflada Larga"), several decisions regarding planning and zoning
will follow.
Mr' Monahan owns four parcels in Ventura that are located in the Westside community
that is included in the Project. The value of each is at least $2,000. The parcels are used for
light-industrial and commercial purposes. His parcels are 17,800 feet (over three miles) from the
potential annexation of Caflada Larga.
You have asked whether Mr. Monahan can participate in decisions related to the
annexation while the Project is on-going and when it is complete.
ANALYSIS
Section 87|00 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or
using his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in which the official has a
financial interest. A public official has a "finan"iul irt"."st" in a g";";;;;ioi a""irior, within
the meaning of the Act, if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material
financial effect on one 1l more of the public official's economic interests. (Section g7I03;
Regulation 18700(a).) The Commission has adopted an eighrstep standard analysis for deciding
whether an individual has a disqualifying conflict of intereit in a given governmental decision.
Steps One and Two: Is Mr. Monahan a "public official" making or participating in making
a governmental decision?
A "public official" is "every member, officer, employee or consultant of a state or local
government agency. - .." (Section 82048.) Mr. Monahan is a public official by virtue of his
position on the ventura City Council. Mr. Monahan will be oik"a b participate in the decision to
support or oppose the LAFCo's annexing the Caflada Largaparcel. Foilowing that, the city
council will also be asked to make several decisions relate-cl to the project. Mi tvtonahan will
therefore be making, participating in making, or influencing a governmental decision . (See
generally Regulation 187 02.)


File No. I-10-095
Page No. 3
step Three: what are Mr. Monahan's Economic Interests?
Mr' Monahan has an economic interest in the real estate he owns in ventura. He owns
four properties in the westside area of ventura, part of the Project. The Act states that a public
official has an economic interest in real propertyin which he or she has a direct or indirect
interest of $2,000 or more' (Section 87103(b); h.egulation 1s703.2.) Mr. Monahan rherefore has
an economic interest in this property.
Step Four: Are the economic interests directly or indirectly involved in the governmental
decisions?
Regulation 18704.2(a)(l) (copy enclosed) states, in pertinent part, that real property in
which a public official has an."oromic interest is rtireitlyinvolved in a governmental decision
if:
"The real property in which the official has an interest, or any part of
that real property, is located within 500 feet of the boundaries (o,. tt "
proposed boundaries) of the property which is the subject of the
governmental decision. For purposes of subdivision (a)(s), reat
property is located 'within 500 feet of the boundaries (orproposed
boundaries) of the real property which is the subject of the
governmental decision' if any part of the real property is within 500
feet of the boundaries (or proposed boundaries) oi ttre redevelopment
project trea.,,,
Mr' Monahan's property is 17,800 feet away from the property line of the potential
lnnex' For purposes of Regulation 18704.2(a)(1;, his properties are not directly involved. while
there is a project occurring in the city, and.on" it ot the city ;;rlJltk;;l;;;, 
you srared
during our telephone conversations that this project has not been designated a .iredevelopment
plan"or "redevelopment project." Because Mr. Monahan's property is not within 500 feet and is
not otherwise "directly" involved, it is indirectly involved.3
Steps Five and Six: Will there be a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on
Mr. Monahan's economic interest?
The financial effect of a govemmental decision on real property that is indirectly
involved in the governmental decision is presumed not to be material. This presumption may be
2 "Ifapublicofficial'seconomicinterestisnotdirectlyinvolvedinagovernmental 
decision,itisindirectly
involved." (Regularion I 8704(a).)
r Regulation 18704'2(a)(2)discusses instances in which zoning or re-zoning would cause the property to be
"directly involved"' Two points on this topic: tirst, tbr that to be the caie, the public ottrcial's property must be the
property subject to the zoning and second, the decision at hand is whether to annex a property that is 3 miles away,
not what zoning designation to give a particular property.


File No. I-10-095
Page No.4
rebutted by proof that there are specific circumstances regarding the governmental decision, its
financial effect' and the nature of the real property whicf,mak"lt r.oionobly foreseeable that the
decision will have a material financial effect on tt " 
real property in which the public official has
an interest' (Regulation 18705.2(b)(l).) Examples of ru"l, rp""iric circLrmsranfes include the
development potential or income producing poiential of the ieal property, the use of the real
property and the character of the neighborhood, including substantial efiects on traffic, view,
privacy, intensity of use, noise levels, and air emissiorr. "H.ra, 
none of the circumstances set
forth in Regulation 18705.2(b) appear to be present to rebut the presumption of non-materiality.
consequently, based on the facts provided, we see no evidence that there would be a reasonabiy
foreseeable material financial effect of the annexation or decisions about the annexation of
Caflada Larga to the Project.
We understand that the city council will be having future discussions regarding the
annexation and the Project. You are welcome to inquireigain should an issue arise.
Steps Seven and Eight: Does this governmental decision come within any exception to the
confl ict-of-interest rules?
The steps above revealed that Mr. Monahan is not barred from participating in a vote on
the caflada Larga annexation or other decisions regarding the annexation. Even if an exception
was applicable, and none is based on your facts, none is iecessary.
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
\^^\h- t'lfrA^r'a",-
By: Heather M. Rowan
Counsel, Legal Division
HMR jgl

QUESTION: Does Ventura City Councilmember James Monahan have a conflict of interest that would
bar him from participating in governmental decisions that involve both annexing a parcel of
property to an existing city project (and other decisions regarding that annexation) where his
property is over three miles from the potential annex?

CONCLUSION: No' Based on the facts you provided in both your letter and our telephone conversations,
Mr' Monahan's economic interest in his real property do., not bar him from participating in
these decisions.
